channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Debate Rages as Men's Rights Advocates Call for Review of Sexual Offences Law
item.description: A fresh wave of discussion is happening in Kenya regarding the country’s sexual offences law. Parliament is preparing to debate amendments to the Sexual Offences (Amendment) Bill, 2023, sponsored by K...

item.title: Young Golfer Felix Mrema Wins Junior Division at Arusha Diplomatic Golf Tournament
item.description: ARUSHA: Felix Mrema, the 17-year-old son of former Arusha-Urban MP Felix Mrema, showed impressive skills at the 7th Diplomatic Charity Golf Tournament held over the weekend at Kiligolf Club. Represent...

item.title: Ramaphosa raises concerns over excessive use of consultants in municipalities
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has sharply criticised municipalities for over-reliance on external consultants, warning that it is eroding the internal capacity of municipal staff and placing financial pre...

item.title: 76-Year-Old British Broadcast Engineer Found Dead in Mumbai Hotel Room During IPL 2026 Duties
item.description: A 76-year-old British national named Ian Williams Langford, also referred to as Jan William Langford in some reports, who was working as a broadcast engineer with the Board of Control for Cricket in I...

item.title: Three teenagers appear in court accused of murdering 16-year-old Chloe Dransfield in Leeds
item.description: Three young people have been charged in connection with the fatal stabbing of 16-year-old Chloe Watson Dransfield in Leeds. West Yorkshire Police confirmed that Kayla Smith, aged 18, and Archie Rycrof...

item.title: KSE-100 recovers over 1,800 points after previous day slump
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange witnessed a notable recovery on Tuesday as the KSE-100 index surged by 1,804.59 points, which is an increase of 1.23 per cent, bringing it to 148,647.56 by midday. This reb...

item.title: Calls to Cancel Israel’s Ambassador Speech at National Press Club Spark Debate
item.description: Today, the National Press Club in Canberra hosted Hillel Newman, Israel’s recently appointed ambassador to Australia, as a special guest. The event, which was broadcast live by the ABC, has sparked co...

item.title: Controversial mural in Brisbane prompts police attention amid political and AI debates
item.description: A recent mural in Brisbane depicting John Farnham with flowing hair and watermelons has stirred controversy due to the inclusion of the phrase “from the river to the sea,” which is now considered ille...

item.title: Council of Governors says Sakaja arrest orders were unjust
item.description: The Council of Governors (CoG) has come out strongly saying that the move to target Nairobi Governor Sakaja for arrest was unfair and lacked proper justification. According to the CoG, singling out on...

item.title: Pakistan Explores China's Role as Guarantor for Possible Iran-US Negotiations
item.description: China is emerging as a potential guarantor in future talks between Iran and the United States, according to diplomatic experts, as Pakistan actively engages in efforts to facilitate a framework for ne...

item.title: The Importance of Building and Maintaining an Emergency Fund in Uncertain Times
item.description: Amid global conflicts, rising living costs, and economic uncertainty, Canadian experts stress the critical importance of having an emergency fund. Such a fund can provide financial security during une...

item.title: Australia Defeats Curacao 5-1 in Friendly Match at Melbourne's AAMI Park
item.description: The Socceroos put on a remarkable performance against Curacao in a friendly match at Melbourne's AAMI Park, dominating the second half to finish with a 5-1 victory. After a balanced first half, Austra...

item.title: Precise Lighting and Legrand Partner to Improve Luxury Building Performance in Nigeria
item.description: On March 24, 2026, for Lekki Phase 1, Lagos, Precise Lighting enter into strategic partnership with Legrand, the global expert for electrical and digital building infrastructure. This collaboration na...

item.title: Police Investigating Alleged Sexual Assault of Woman in Rawat Area
item.description: RAWALPINDI: A tragic incident has been reported from the Baga Sheikhan area near Rawat, Punjab, where a 19-year-old woman was allegedly gang-raped by four men while travelling in a moving van. Accordi...

item.title: Controller of Budget flags risk of government debt default over Sh3 trillion
item.description: Kenya is staring at a serious debt problem, with the government potentially defaulting on Sh3.32 trillion of external debt due within the next year, warns Controller of Budget Margaret Nyakang’o. She ...

item.title: Radio doctor charged with 102 counts for failure to file tax and VAT returns
item.description: Dr Leonard Mandlalele Mhinga, aged 73 and known for his radio broadcasts, has been formally charged with 102 offences for failing to submit both income tax and value-added tax (VAT) returns over a pro...

item.title: Michelle Tsiakkas departs from Strictly Come Dancing ahead of 2026 series
item.description: Strictly Come Dancing has confirmed that professional dancer Michelle Tsiakkas will not be returning for the 2026 series following a cast overhaul. Alongside previous departures of Karen Hauer, Nadiya...

item.title: Japan Deploys Long-Range Missiles to Strengthen National Defence
item.description: Japan has deployed upgraded Type-12 land-to-ship missiles at Camp Kengun in Kumamoto prefecture, marking a significant enhancement of its offensive capabilities. These missiles, developed by Mitsubish...

item.title: Senator Imee Marcos expresses regret to VP Sara Duterte over 2022 election involvement and political fallout
item.description: Senator Imee Marcos publicly apologized to Vice President Sara Duterte for persuading her to run as vice presidential candidate in the 2022 national elections under the Uniteam alliance with President...

item.title: Botswana’s Economy Contracts Due to Mining Sector Downturn
item.description: Botswana’s economy faced a notable contraction in the final quarter of 2025, with real GDP declining by 5.4% year-on-year. The main culprit behind this downturn was the mining and quarrying sector, wh...
